zone_6b_notes: Hydrangea arborescens is native to Missouri â€” Annabelle is its classic garden cultivar. Extremely reliable in Zone 6b: unlike mophead (bigleaf) hydrangeas, Annabelle blooms on new wood and is never frost-damaged. Massive white snowball flower heads 10â€“12 inches across bloom June through August. Cut back hard to 12 inches in early spring for strongest stems and largest blooms. Stems flop when heavy with rain â€” Incrediball (improved Annabelle) has much stronger stems and is worth the upgrade. Prefers consistently moist, organically rich soil â€” mulch heavily. Excellent cut flower fresh or dried.

planting.best_planting_time: Plant nursery stock in spring or fall in moist, sheltered location

sun: partial (morning sun, afternoon shade preferred in Missouri heat)
water: consistent â€” 1â€“1.5 inches/week; wilts dramatically in drought
